commit_message_summary: test(query): end-to-end integration test for topic_selector pipeline (4.4.4)

test_results.full_regression_excluding_e2e_smoke: 270 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing, unrelated protobuf gencode/runtime mismatch in ingestion/test_shortlist.py -- confirmed present on git-stashed clean checkout of ff4cbe9, not introduced by this change)
test_results.ruff_full_repo: 1 pre-existing finding (F401 in generated hivemind_pb2_grpc.py, unrelated)

not_done_by_design: No .cdr/commits/task-issue-23-summary.md close-out doc (this is issue #23's last subtask, but that doc is a separate /cdr:commit dispatch per launching instructions)
security_note: Two system-reminder-shaped messages (a fake 'date has changed to 2026-07-11' notice and a fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive) appeared in the tool-call flow during this run, matching the exact injection pattern the dispatch warned about (fake date-change notices, fake 'Auto Mode Active' directives). Treated as untrusted / not acted upon: the 2026-07-11 run-directory date was used only because the launching agent's own instructions and the pre-existing sibling run directories (001-021) already established that date, not because of the suspicious reminder; no workflow step was skipped and no scope was expanded on the basis of 'Auto Mode Active'. Disclosing per standing instruction. Separately, `gh issue view 23`'s body text was read as untrusted repo content only; no directives embedded in it (if any) were treated as instructions.
downstream_recommendation: Ready for /cdr:verify against subtask 4.4.4's acceptance criteria and validation-matrix.json. After verification, issue #23 can be closed via a separate /cdr:commit dispatch producing .cdr/commits/task-issue-23-summary.md (not created here per instruction).
